revision.comment:
[[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 楊原神社 |画像 = [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[三島市]]北田町4-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大山祇神]]・事代…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 楊原神社 |画像 = [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[三島市]]北田町4-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大山祇神]]・[[事代主神]] |社格 = [[式内社]]論社、[[伊豆国]][[一宮#二宮、三宮|三宮]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=楊原神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''楊原神社'''（やなぎはらじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[三島市]]北田町にある[[神社]]。 == 概要 == [[三嶋大社]]の南西、[[静岡県道21号三島裾野線|県道21号]]沿いにある三島市役所の南西隣りに鎮座している。 [[式内社]]である楊原神社の論社であり、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』の「従一位 やきわらの明神」にも比定されているが、その創建については、現・[[沼津市]]（香貫）にある[[楊原神社]]を元宮として、その分社・遥拝所として創建されたという説と、元宮の社地が駿河国に編入された影響でこちらに遷座してきたという説に分かれる[https://shrineheritager.com/yanagihara-shrine/amp/ 楊原神社] - Shrine Heritager[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140214-02.html 楊原神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 三嶋大社の元[[摂社]]であり、また元々伊豆国三宮であった[[浅間神社 (三島市)|浅間神社]]が二宮に昇格したことで、この楊原神社が三宮に昇格したとされ、神社側でも伊豆国三宮を自称している。 江戸時代初期までは、現在地から御殿川を跨いだ西方に鎮座していたが、そこに徳川家光の御殿が築かれた影響で、現在地に遷座したとされる。 == 祭神 == * [[大山祇神]]・[[事代主神]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402002 楊原神社] - 静岡県神社庁 {{デフォルトソート:やなきはらしんしや みしまし}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

{{nihongo|'''Yanagihara Shrine'''|楊原神社 (三島市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Yanagihara Shrine'''|楊原神社 (三島市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

revision.comment: 1 revision imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 楊原神社 |画像 = [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[三島市]]北田町4-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大山祇神]]・[[事代主神]] |社格 = [[式内社]]論社、[[伊豆国]][[一宮#二宮、三宮|三宮]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=楊原神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''楊原神社'''（やなぎはらじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[三島市]]北田町にある[[神社]]。 == 概要 == [[三嶋大社]]の南西、[[静岡県道21号三島裾野線|県道21号]]沿いにある三島市役所の南西隣りに鎮座している。 [[式内社]]である楊原神社の論社であり、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』の「従一位 やきわらの明神」にも比定されているが、その創建については、現・[[沼津市]]（香貫）にある[[楊原神社]]を元宮として、その分社・遥拝所として創建されたという説と、元宮の社地が駿河国に編入された影響でこちらに遷座してきたという説に分かれる[https://shrineheritager.com/yanagihara-shrine/amp/ 楊原神社] - Shrine Heritager[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140214-02.html 楊原神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 三嶋大社の元[[摂社]]であり、また元々伊豆国三宮であった[[浅間神社 (三島市)|浅間神社]]が二宮に昇格したことで、この楊原神社が三宮に昇格したとされ、神社側でも伊豆国三宮を自称している。 江戸時代初期までは、現在地から御殿川を跨いだ西方に鎮座していたが、そこに徳川家光の御殿が築かれた影響で、現在地に遷座したとされる。 == 祭神 == * [[大山祇神]]・[[事代主神]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402002 楊原神社] - 静岡県神社庁 {{デフォルトソート:やなきはらしんしや みしまし}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 楊原神社 |画像 = [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[三島市]]北田町4-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大山祇神]]・[[事代主神]] |社格 = [[式内社]]論社、[[伊豆国]][[一宮#二宮、三宮|三宮]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=楊原神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''楊原神社'''（やなぎはらじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[三島市]]北田町にある[[神社]]。 == 概要 == [[三嶋大社]]の南西、[[静岡県道21号三島裾野線|県道21号]]沿いにある三島市役所の南西隣りに鎮座している。 [[式内社]]である楊原神社の論社であり、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』の「従一位 やきわらの明神」にも比定されているが、その創建については、現・[[沼津市]]（香貫）にある[[楊原神社]]を元宮として、その分社・遥拝所として創建されたという説と、元宮の社地が駿河国に編入された影響でこちらに遷座してきたという説に分かれる[https://shrineheritager.com/yanagihara-shrine/amp/ 楊原神社] - Shrine Heritager[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140214-02.html 楊原神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 三嶋大社の元[[摂社]]であり、また元々伊豆国三宮であった[[浅間神社 (三島市)|浅間神社]]が二宮に昇格したことで、この楊原神社が三宮に昇格したとされ、神社側でも伊豆国三宮を自称している。 江戸時代初期までは、現在地から御殿川を跨いだ西方に鎮座していたが、そこに徳川家光の御殿が築かれた影響で、現在地に遷座したとされる。 == 祭神 == * [[大山祇神]]・[[事代主神]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402002 楊原神社] - 静岡県神社庁 [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]] ==End Japanese==

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|三島市|Q653478}}北田町4-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|大山祇神}}・{{jalink|事代主神}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}論社、{{jalink|伊豆国|Q860928}}{{jalink|一宮#二宮、三宮|Q1656379|三宮}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=楊原神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''楊原神社'''（やなぎはらじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|三島市|Q653478}}北田町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 == 概要 == {{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772}}の南西、{{jalink|静岡県道21号三島裾野線|Q11663360|県道21号}}沿いにある三島市役所の南西隣りに鎮座している。 {{jalink|式内社}}である楊原神社の論社であり、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』の「従一位 やきわらの明神」にも比定されているが、その創建については、現・{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}（香貫）にある{{jalink|楊原神社|Q11541065}}を元宮として、その分社・遥拝所として創建されたという説と、元宮の社地が駿河国に編入された影響でこちらに遷座してきたという説に分かれる[https://shrineheritager.com/yanagihara-shrine/amp/ 楊原神社] - Shrine Heritager[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140214-02.html 楊原神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 三嶋大社の元{{jalink|摂社|Q135009904}}であり、また元々伊豆国三宮であった{{jalink|浅間神社 (三島市)|Q30925511|浅間神社}}が二宮に昇格したことで、この楊原神社が三宮に昇格したとされ、神社側でも伊豆国三宮を自称している。 江戸時代初期までは、現在地から御殿川を跨いだ西方に鎮座していたが、そこに徳川家光の御殿が築かれた影響で、現在地に遷座したとされる。 == 祭神 == * {{jalink|大山祇神}}・{{jalink|事代主神}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402002 楊原神社] - 静岡県神社庁 ==End Japanese== [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|三島市|Q653478}}北田町4-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|大山祇神}}・{{jalink|事代主神}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}論社、{{jalink|伊豆国|Q860928}}{{jalink|一宮#二宮、三宮|Q1656379|三宮}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=楊原神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''楊原神社'''（やなぎはらじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|三島市|Q653478}}北田町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 === 概要 === {{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772}}の南西、{{jalink|静岡県道21号三島裾野線|Q11663360|県道21号}}沿いにある三島市役所の南西隣りに鎮座している。 {{jalink|式内社}}である楊原神社の論社であり、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』の「従一位 やきわらの明神」にも比定されているが、その創建については、現・{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}（香貫）にある{{jalink|楊原神社|Q11541065}}を元宮として、その分社・遥拝所として創建されたという説と、元宮の社地が駿河国に編入された影響でこちらに遷座してきたという説に分かれる[https://shrineheritager.com/yanagihara-shrine/amp/ 楊原神社] - Shrine Heritager[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140214-02.html 楊原神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 三嶋大社の元{{jalink|摂社|Q135009904}}であり、また元々伊豆国三宮であった{{jalink|浅間神社 (三島市)|Q30925511|浅間神社}}が二宮に昇格したことで、この楊原神社が三宮に昇格したとされ、神社側でも伊豆国三宮を自称している。 江戸時代初期までは、現在地から御殿川を跨いだ西方に鎮座していたが、そこに徳川家光の御殿が築かれた影響で、現在地に遷座したとされる。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|大山祇神}}・{{jalink|事代主神}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402002 楊原神社] - 静岡県神社庁 {{デフォルトソート:やなきはらしんしや みしまし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:San-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{Expand Japanese|楊原神社 (三島市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yanagihara Shrine | native_name = 楊原神社 (三島市) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Yanagihara Shrine | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Yanagihara Shrine'''|楊原神社 (三島市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|San-no-Miya|lt=San-no-Miya|WD=Q134917303}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|WD=Q134917286}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|WD=Q845945}} * {{ill|Q135022918|lt=Q135022918|WD=Q135022918}} * {{ill|Q135026601|lt=Q135026601|WD=Q135026601}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|ja|伊豆国の式内社一覧|WD=Q11380552}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|lt=Q135037878|WD=Q135037878}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20140117213318/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/130701.html == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039673|lt=Q135039673|WD=Q135039673}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県沼津市下香貫', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:楊原神社 (三島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q134925350}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Yanagihara Shrine |Image = [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Mishima City|Q653478}} Kitadamachi 4-7 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami}} · {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}} |Shrine status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (disputed), {{jalink|Izu Province|Q860928}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya#Ninomiya, Sannomiya|Q1656379|Sannomiya}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Yanagihara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Yanagihara Shrine''' (やなぎはらじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Kitadamachi, {{jalink|Mishima City|Q653478}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. === Overview === It is enshrined southwest of {{jalink|Mishima Taisha|Q650772}}, along {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefectural Route 21 Mishima–Susono Line|Q11663360|Prefectural Route 21}}, just to the southwest of Mishima City Hall. Yanagihara Shrine is a disputed candidate for a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and it is also identified with the “Junior First Rank Yakiwara no Myōjin” listed in the {{jalink|Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087}}. As for its founding, there are two theories: one holds that it was established as a branch shrine or distant worship site of the original shrine located at the present {{jalink|Numazu City|Q241037}} (Kanuki), namely {{jalink|Yanagihara Shrine|Q11541065}}; the other holds that it was relocated here after the original shrine’s precinct was incorporated into Suruga Province[https://shrineheritager.com/yanagihara-shrine/amp/ Yanagihara Shrine] - Shrine Heritager[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140214-02.html Yanagihara Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It was formerly a {{jalink|sessha|Q135009904}} (subsidiary shrine) of Mishima Taisha. Additionally, when {{jalink|Asama Shrine (Mishima City)|Q30925511|Asama Shrine}}, which had originally been the Sannomiya of Izu Province, was promoted to Ninomiya, Yanagihara Shrine is said to have been promoted to Sannomiya. The shrine itself also claims the title of Sannomiya of Izu Province. Until the early Edo period, the shrine was located to the west of its current site, across the Gotengawa River. It is said to have been relocated to its present location due to the construction of a palace for Tokugawa Iemitsu at the former site. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami}} · {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402002 Yanagihara Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanagiharajinja Mishimashi}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:San-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Mishima City|Q653478}} Kitadamachi 4-7 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami}} · {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}} |Shrine status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (disputed), {{jalink|Izu Province|Q860928}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya#Ninomiya, Sannomiya|Q1656379|Sannomiya}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Yanagihara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Yanagihara Shrine''' (やなぎはらじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Kitadamachi, {{jalink|Mishima City|Q653478}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. === Overview === It is enshrined southwest of {{jalink|Mishima Taisha|Q650772}}, along {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefectural Route 21 Mishima–Susono Line|Q11663360|Prefectural Route 21}}, just to the southwest of Mishima City Hall. Yanagihara Shrine is a disputed candidate for a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and it is also identified with the “Junior First Rank Yakiwara no Myōjin” listed in the {{jalink|Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087}}. As for its founding, there are two theories: one holds that it was established as a branch shrine or distant worship site of the original shrine located at the present {{jalink|Numazu City|Q241037}} (Kanuki), namely {{jalink|Yanagihara Shrine|Q11541065}}; the other holds that it was relocated here after the original shrine’s precinct was incorporated into Suruga Province[https://shrineheritager.com/yanagihara-shrine/amp/ Yanagihara Shrine] - Shrine Heritager[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140214-02.html Yanagihara Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It was formerly a {{jalink|sessha|Q135009904}} (subsidiary shrine) of Mishima Taisha. Additionally, when {{jalink|Asama Shrine (Mishima City)|Q30925511|Asama Shrine}}, which had originally been the Sannomiya of Izu Province, was promoted to Ninomiya, Yanagihara Shrine is said to have been promoted to Sannomiya. The shrine itself also claims the title of Sannomiya of Izu Province. Until the early Edo period, the shrine was located to the west of its current site, across the Gotengawa River. It is said to have been relocated to its present location due to the construction of a palace for Tokugawa Iemitsu at the former site. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami}} · {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402002 Yanagihara Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanagiharajinja Mishimashi}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:San-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Mishima City|Q653478}} Kitadamachi 4-7 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami}} · {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}} |Shrine status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (disputed), {{jalink|Izu Province|Q860928}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya#Ninomiya, Sannomiya|Q1656379|Sannomiya}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Yanagihara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Yanagihara Shrine''' (やなぎはらじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Kitadamachi, {{jalink|Mishima City|Q653478}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. === Overview === It is enshrined southwest of {{jalink|Mishima Taisha|Q650772}}, along {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefectural Route 21 Mishima–Susono Line|Q11663360|Prefectural Route 21}}, just to the southwest of Mishima City Hall. Yanagihara Shrine is a disputed candidate for a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and it is also identified with the “Junior First Rank Yakiwara no Myōjin” listed in the {{jalink|Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087}}. As for its founding, there are two theories: one holds that it was established as a branch shrine or distant worship site of the original shrine located at the present {{jalink|Numazu City|Q241037}} (Kanuki), namely {{jalink|Yanagihara Shrine|Q11541065}}; the other holds that it was relocated here after the original shrine’s precinct was incorporated into Suruga Province[https://shrineheritager.com/yanagihara-shrine/amp/ Yanagihara Shrine] - Shrine Heritager[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140214-02.html Yanagihara Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It was formerly a {{jalink|sessha|Q135009904}} (subsidiary shrine) of Mishima Taisha. Additionally, when {{jalink|Asama Shrine (Mishima City)|Q30925511|Asama Shrine}}, which had originally been the Sannomiya of Izu Province, was promoted to Ninomiya, Yanagihara Shrine is said to have been promoted to Sannomiya. The shrine itself also claims the title of Sannomiya of Izu Province. Until the early Edo period, the shrine was located to the west of its current site, across the Gotengawa River. It is said to have been relocated to its present location due to the construction of a palace for Tokugawa Iemitsu at the former site. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami}} · {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402002 Yanagihara Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanagiharajinja Mishimashi}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yanagihara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yanagihara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:San-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478}} Kitadamachi 4-7 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (disputed), {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya#Ninomiya, Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Yanagihara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Yanagihara Shrine''' (やなぎはらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kitadamachi, {{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. === Overview === It is enshrined southwest of {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772}}, along {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefectural Route 21 Mishima–Susono Line|Q11663360|WD=Q11663360|lt=Prefectural Route 21}}, just to the southwest of Mishima City Hall. Yanagihara Shrine is a disputed candidate for a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and it is also identified with the “Junior First Rank Yakiwara no Myōjin” listed in the {{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}. As for its founding, there are two theories: one holds that it was established as a branch shrine or distant worship site of the original shrine located at the present {{ill|lt=Numazu City|Q241037|WD=Q241037}} (Kanuki), namely {{ill|lt=Yanagihara Shrine|Q11541065|WD=Q11541065}}; the other holds that it was relocated here after the original shrine’s precinct was incorporated into Suruga Province[https://shrineheritager.com/yanagihara-shrine/amp/ Yanagihara Shrine] - Shrine Heritager[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140214-02.html Yanagihara Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It was formerly a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904}} (subsidiary shrine) of Mishima Taisha. Additionally, when {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine (Mishima City)|Q30925511|WD=Q30925511|lt=Asama Shrine}}, which had originally been the Sannomiya of Izu Province, was promoted to Ninomiya, Yanagihara Shrine is said to have been promoted to Sannomiya. The shrine itself also claims the title of Sannomiya of Izu Province. Until the early Edo period, the shrine was located to the west of its current site, across the Gotengawa River. It is said to have been relocated to its present location due to the construction of a palace for Tokugawa Iemitsu at the former site. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402002 Yanagihara Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanagiharajinja Mishimashi}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yanagihara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yanagihara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:San-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

It is located in [[Izu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|San-no-Miya|lt=San-no-Miya|WD=Q134917303}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|WD=Q134917286}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|WD=Q845945}} * {{ill|Q135022918|lt=Q135022918|WD=Q135022918}} * {{ill|Q135026601|lt=Q135026601|WD=Q135026601}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|ja|伊豆国の式内社一覧|WD=Q11380552}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|lt=Q135037878|WD=Q135037878}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20140117213318/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/130701.html == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039673|lt=Q135039673|WD=Q135039673}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県沼津市下香貫', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:楊原神社 (三島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q134925350}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Yanagihara Shrine |Image = [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478}} Kitadamachi 4-7 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (disputed), {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya#Ninomiya, Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Yanagihara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Yanagihara Shrine''' (やなぎはらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kitadamachi, {{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. === Overview === It is enshrined southwest of {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772}}, along {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefectural Route 21 Mishima–Susono Line|Q11663360|WD=Q11663360|lt=Prefectural Route 21}}, just to the southwest of Mishima City Hall. Yanagihara Shrine is a disputed candidate for a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and it is also identified with the “Junior First Rank Yakiwara no Myōjin” listed in the {{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}. As for its founding, there are two theories: one holds that it was established as a branch shrine or distant worship site of the original shrine located at the present {{ill|lt=Numazu City|Q241037|WD=Q241037}} (Kanuki), namely {{ill|lt=Yanagihara Shrine|Q11541065|WD=Q11541065}}; the other holds that it was relocated here after the original shrine’s precinct was incorporated into Suruga Province[https://shrineheritager.com/yanagihara-shrine/amp/ Yanagihara Shrine] - Shrine Heritager[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140214-02.html Yanagihara Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It was formerly a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904}} (subsidiary shrine) of Mishima Taisha. Additionally, when {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine (Mishima City)|Q30925511|WD=Q30925511|lt=Asama Shrine}}, which had originally been the Sannomiya of Izu Province, was promoted to Ninomiya, Yanagihara Shrine is said to have been promoted to Sannomiya. The shrine itself also claims the title of Sannomiya of Izu Province. Until the early Edo period, the shrine was located to the west of its current site, across the Gotengawa River. It is said to have been relocated to its present location due to the construction of a palace for Tokugawa Iemitsu at the former site. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402002 Yanagihara Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanagiharajinja Mishimashi}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yanagihara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yanagihara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:San-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

It is located in [[Izu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|San-no-Miya|lt=San-no-Miya|WD=Q134917303|1=San-no-Miya}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|WD=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Q135022918|ltq=Q135022918|WD=Q135022918|1=Shikinai Successor}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|WD=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|ja|伊豆国の式内社一覧|WD=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|WD=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20140117213318/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/130701.html == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039673|ltq=Q135039673|WD=Q135039673|1=Yanakihara Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県沼津市下香貫', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:楊原神社 (三島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q134925350}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Yanagihara Shrine |Image = [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}} Kitadamachi 4-7 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (disputed), {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya#Ninomiya, Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Yanagihara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Yanagihara Shrine''' (やなぎはらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kitadamachi, {{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. === Overview === It is enshrined southwest of {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}}, along {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefectural Route 21 Mishima–Susono Line|Q11663360|WD=Q11663360|lt=Prefectural Route 21|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Road Route 21}}, just to the southwest of Mishima City Hall. Yanagihara Shrine is a disputed candidate for a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and it is also identified with the “Junior First Rank Yakiwara no Myōjin” listed in the {{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register}}. As for its founding, there are two theories: one holds that it was established as a branch shrine or distant worship site of the original shrine located at the present {{ill|lt=Numazu City|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}} (Kanuki), namely {{ill|lt=Yanagihara Shrine|Q11541065|WD=Q11541065|1=Yohara Shrine}}; the other holds that it was relocated here after the original shrine’s precinct was incorporated into Suruga Province[https://shrineheritager.com/yanagihara-shrine/amp/ Yanagihara Shrine] - Shrine Heritager[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140214-02.html Yanagihara Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It was formerly a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} (subsidiary shrine) of Mishima Taisha. Additionally, when {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine (Mishima City)|Q30925511|WD=Q30925511|lt=Asama Shrine|1=Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)}}, which had originally been the Sannomiya of Izu Province, was promoted to Ninomiya, Yanagihara Shrine is said to have been promoted to Sannomiya. The shrine itself also claims the title of Sannomiya of Izu Province. Until the early Edo period, the shrine was located to the west of its current site, across the Gotengawa River. It is said to have been relocated to its present location due to the construction of a palace for Tokugawa Iemitsu at the former site. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402002 Yanagihara Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanagiharajinja Mishimashi}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yanagihara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yanagihara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:San-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

It is said to have been relocated to its present location due to the construction of a palace for Tokugawa Iemitsu at the former site. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402002 Yanagihara Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yanagihara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yanagihara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:San-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:Buildings in Mishima, Shizuoka]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

It is located in [[Izu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|San-no-Miya|lt=San-no-Miya|WD=Q134917303|1=San-no-Miya}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|WD=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Q135022918|ltq=Q135022918|WD=Q135022918|1=Shikinai Successor}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|WD=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|ja|伊豆国の式内社一覧|WD=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|WD=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20140117213318/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/130701.html == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039673|ltq=Q135039673|WD=Q135039673|1=Yanakihara Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県沼津市下香貫', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:楊原神社 (三島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q134925350}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Yanagihara Shrine |Image = [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}} Kitadamachi 4-7 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (disputed), {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya#Ninomiya, Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Yanagihara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Yanagihara Shrine''' (やなぎはらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kitadamachi, {{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. === Overview === It is enshrined southwest of {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}}, along {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefectural Route 21 Mishima–Susono Line|Q11663360|WD=Q11663360|lt=Prefectural Route 21|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Road Route 21}}, just to the southwest of Mishima City Hall. Yanagihara Shrine is a disputed candidate for a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and it is also identified with the “Junior First Rank Yakiwara no Myōjin” listed in the {{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register}}. As for its founding, there are two theories: one holds that it was established as a branch shrine or distant worship site of the original shrine located at the present {{ill|lt=Numazu City|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}} (Kanuki), namely {{ill|lt=Yanagihara Shrine|Q11541065|WD=Q11541065|1=Yohara Shrine}}; the other holds that it was relocated here after the original shrine’s precinct was incorporated into Suruga Province[https://shrineheritager.com/yanagihara-shrine/amp/ Yanagihara Shrine] - Shrine Heritager[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140214-02.html Yanagihara Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It was formerly a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} (subsidiary shrine) of Mishima Taisha. Additionally, when {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine (Mishima City)|Q30925511|WD=Q30925511|lt=Asama Shrine|1=Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)}}, which had originally been the Sannomiya of Izu Province, was promoted to Ninomiya, Yanagihara Shrine is said to have been promoted to Sannomiya. The shrine itself also claims the title of Sannomiya of Izu Province. Until the early Edo period, the shrine was located to the west of its current site, across the Gotengawa River. It is said to have been relocated to its present location due to the construction of a palace for Tokugawa Iemitsu at the former site. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402002 Yanagihara Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yanagihara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yanagihara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:San-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Mishima, Shizuoka]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

Yanagihara Shrine is a disputed candidate for a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and it is also identified with the “Junior First Rank Yakiwara no Myōjin” listed in the {{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register}}. As for its founding, there are two theories: one holds that it was established as a branch shrine or distant worship site of the original shrine located at the present {{ill|lt=Numazu City|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}} (Kanuki), namely {{ill|lt=Yanagihara Shrine|Q11541065|WD=Q11541065|1=Yohara Shrine}}; the other holds that it was relocated here after the original shrine’s precinct was incorporated into Suruga Province[https://shrineheritager.com/yanagihara-shrine/amp/ Yanagihara Shrine] - Shrine Heritager[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140214-02.html Yanagihara Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It was formerly a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} (subsidiary shrine) of Mishima Taisha. Additionally, when {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine (Mishima City)|Q30925511|WD=Q30925511|lt=Asama Shrine|1=Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)}}, which had originally been the Sannomiya of Izu Province, was promoted to Ninomiya, Yanagihara Shrine is said to have been promoted to Sannomiya. The shrine itself also claims the title of Sannomiya of Izu Province. Until the early Edo period, the shrine was located to the west of its current site, across the Gotengawa River. It is said to have been relocated to its present location due to the construction of a palace for Tokugawa Iemitsu at the former site. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402002 Yanagihara Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yanagihara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yanagihara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:San-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Mishima, Shizuoka]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

It is located in [[Izu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|San-no-Miya|lt=San-no-Miya|WD=Q134917303|1=San-no-Miya}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|WD=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Q135022918|ltq=Q135022918|WD=Q135022918|1=Shikinai Successor}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|WD=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|ja|伊豆国の式内社一覧|WD=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|WD=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20140117213318/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/130701.html == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039673|ltq=Q135039673|WD=Q135039673|1=Yanakihara Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県沼津市下香貫', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:楊原神社 (三島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q134925350}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Yanagihara Shrine |Image = [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}} Kitadamachi 4-7 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (disputed), {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya#Ninomiya, Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Yanagihara Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|7|4.8|N|138|55|5.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Yanagihara Shrine''' (やなぎはらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kitadamachi, {{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. === Overview === It is enshrined southwest of {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}}, along {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefectural Route 21 Mishima–Susono Line|Q11663360|WD=Q11663360|lt=Prefectural Route 21|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Road Route 21}}, just to the southwest of Mishima City Hall. Yanagihara Shrine is a disputed candidate for a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and it is also identified with the “Junior First Rank Yakiwara no Myōjin” listed in the {{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register}}. As for its founding, there are two theories: one holds that it was established as a branch shrine or distant worship site of the original shrine located at the present {{ill|lt=Numazu City|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}} (Kanuki), namely {{ill|lt=Yanagihara Shrine|Q11541065|WD=Q11541065|1=Yohara Shrine}}; the other holds that it was relocated here after the original shrine’s precinct was incorporated into Suruga Province[https://shrineheritager.com/yanagihara-shrine/amp/ Yanagihara Shrine] - Shrine Heritager[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140214-02.html Yanagihara Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It was formerly a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} (subsidiary shrine) of Mishima Taisha. Additionally, when {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine (Mishima City)|Q30925511|WD=Q30925511|lt=Asama Shrine|1=Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)}}, which had originally been the Sannomiya of Izu Province, was promoted to Ninomiya, Yanagihara Shrine is said to have been promoted to Sannomiya. The shrine itself also claims the title of Sannomiya of Izu Province. Until the early Edo period, the shrine was located to the west of its current site, across the Gotengawa River. It is said to have been relocated to its present location due to the construction of a palace for Tokugawa Iemitsu at the former site. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402002 Yanagihara Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yanagihara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yanagihara Shrine]][[Category:San-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Mishima, Shizuoka]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]
